claim: The `if` field requires Claude Code v2.1.85 or later (released March 27, 2026); earlier versions silently ignore it and run the hook on every matched call
evidence: Official changelog entry: 'Added conditional `if` field for hooks using permission rule syntax to filter when they run'

claim: The `if` field is placed on individual hook handler objects (innermost hooks array), not on the matcher group
evidence: Official hooks-guide documentation with JSON structure example

claim: `if` is only evaluated on PreToolUse, PostToolUse, PostToolUseFailure, and PermissionRequest; on all other events a hook with `if` set never runs — silently
evidence: Official docs: 'Adding it to any other event prevents the hook from running'

claim: The `if` field uses permission rule syntax (e.g., Bash(git *), Edit(*.ts)) and works with ALL tool types — not just Bash
evidence: Official hooks-guide: 'The `if` field accepts the same patterns as permission rules: Bash(git *), Edit(*.ts), and so on'; Gemini 2.5 Pro agreed

claim: The `if` field is evaluated before the hook process spawns; when condition fails no subprocess is created — this is the primary performance benefit
evidence: v2.1.85 changelog: 'significantly reducing unnecessary process spawning overhead'

claim: Bash(git *) with a space before * enforces a word boundary: matches 'git commit' but not 'gitignore'
evidence: Official permissions docs on wildcard behavior

claim: Bash(git push *) does NOT match bare 'git push' with no arguments because the space before * requires a suffix
evidence: Pattern space-before-wildcard word boundary behavior documented in official permissions reference

claim: File path patterns in `if` follow gitignore spec: // = absolute, ~/ = home-relative, / = project-root-relative, ./ or bare = cwd-relative
evidence: Official permissions docs path prefix table

claim: On Windows, file paths are normalized to POSIX form before `if` matching: C:\Users\alice becomes /c/Users/alice; use //c/**/.env for drive-relative patterns
evidence: Official permissions docs on Windows path normalization; GV1 confirmed with direct doc quote

claim: OR syntax via pipe within a single `if` value (e.g., 'Bash(git *)|Bash(npm *)') is unconfirmed by official docs but demonstrably working in this project (P14 commit-tracker)
evidence: Official docs recommend separate handlers; P14 uses pipe syntax in production; Gemini independent assessment: agreed (working-but-undocumented)

claim: Negation syntax (!, NOT, ^) for the `if` field is not documented and almost certainly not supported
evidence: Absence from official docs; no community examples found

claim: This project has 17 project-level hooks and 4 user-level hooks (HOME locale)
evidence: Direct read of .claude/settings.json and ~/.claude/settings.json

claim: Three hooks received `if` conditions this session: block-push-to-main.js (Bash(git push *)), pre-commit-agent-compliance.js (Bash(git commit *)), commit-tracker.js (multi-pattern)
evidence: Direct read of .claude/settings.json

claim: The three `if`-conditioned hooks save an estimated 25–130 seconds of cumulative spawn overhead per session
evidence: Estimated from Bash call frequency (~50–150/session) and spawn cost (~200–300ms)

claim: gsd-context-monitor.js (U2) has ~92% unnecessary spawn rate — fires on ALL PostToolUse events, exits immediately when context > 35%
evidence: Direct script analysis: bail-out #4 at line 66 fires on ~92% of main-session calls; Gemini agreed

claim: D7-performance.md's claim that `if` only works with Bash-matcher hooks was an inductive fallacy; official docs explicitly confirm `if` works with Edit(*.ts) and all tool types
evidence: Official hooks-guide: 'The `if` field accepts the same patterns as permission rules: Bash(git *), Edit(*.ts), and so on'

claim: The remaining 11 hooks without `if` conditions either cannot use `if` (wrong event type) or internal bail-outs are already more efficient than any glob pattern
evidence: Per-hook analysis of all 11 hooks; post-write-validator.js uses isJsTsFile/isTsxFile/isTestFile boolean dispatch that cannot be expressed as glob

claim: post-write-validator.js was explicitly consolidated from 10 hooks to save ~800ms Windows spawn overhead; splitting it with `if`-based hooks would partially restore that cost
evidence: File header comment lines 6-20; ~80ms per additional hook on Windows; GV1 confirmed Gemini's counter-argument only applies to 1-validator file types, not the .ts/.tsx majority

claim: gsd-context-monitor.js is completely tool-agnostic — it does not inspect tool_name, tool_input, or tool_output; only reads session_id and cwd
evidence: Full script read of gsd-context-monitor.js lines 1-141; no reference to tool_name or similar fields

claim: gsd-context-monitor.js measured spawn cost: 179ms median per invocation on this machine (vs 234ms for ensure-fnm.sh hooks)
evidence: Direct timing: 5 runs (196, 178, 179, 199, 158ms)

claim: Adding broad tool matcher to gsd-context-monitor.js excluding Read/Grep/Glob yields ~48% spawn reduction with low risk of delayed warnings
evidence: Estimated from per-tool fire rates: Read ~100/session, Grep ~20, Glob ~15 excluded of ~259 total

claim: The D4 suggestion 'if: {{ context_window.remaining_percentage }} < 40' is definitively NOT supported — the `if` field is a static pattern matcher with no access to session state
evidence: Official docs: `if` uses permission rule syntax only; no template expressions; 5 open GitHub issues request this capability confirming it does not exist; GV2 independently confirmed

claim: Bash-only matcher for gsd-context-monitor is unacceptable — creates dangerous blind spots where context exhaustion goes undetected during non-Bash execution phases
evidence: Session model: 110+ non-Bash tool calls possible between Bash fires; could represent 30-40% context consumption unmonitored

claim: ensure-fnm.sh adds 167ms overhead per hook invocation on this machine — two fnm binary spawns (fnm env ~80ms + fnm use ~70ms)
evidence: 10-run average measurement: ensure-fnm.sh 233ms vs bare node 66ms = 167ms overhead; Gemini agreed with version-drift caveat

claim: node IS already on PATH without ensure-fnm.sh on this machine via fnm/aliases/default in Windows User environment registry
evidence: PowerShell PATH registry query confirmed fnm/aliases/default present; which node resolves in bare bash --norc session

claim: The fnm use --silent-if-unchanged call is a no-op on every invocation: .nvmrc pins v22, fnm default alias is v22.22.1 — same version
evidence: Direct read of .nvmrc (contains '22') and which node resolving to v22.22.1 installation

claim: A lean PATH-check wrapper skipping fnm init when node is already available reduces per-spawn cost from ~234ms to ~5ms with maintained WORK locale safety
evidence: Proposed Option B: 'if command -v node; then exec $@; fi' pattern with fnm slow-path fallback

claim: At typical 336 hook invocations per session, ensure-fnm.sh redundant overhead costs ~56 seconds — exceeding the ~25-130s savings from all three `if` conditions combined
evidence: 336 invocations × 167ms = 56 seconds; session invocation count estimated from D7 per-hook fire rates

claim: All three existing `if` conditions are locale-safe at both HOME and WORK: git is in Git Bash PATH everywhere, pattern engine requires no external tools
evidence: Direct inspection of .claude/settings.json conditions; cross-referenced with D1-spec path separator analysis

claim: D8-locale's warning that file-path `if` patterns fail on Windows due to backslash escape chars is refuted for the `if` field — POSIX normalization applies before matching
evidence: Official permissions docs: 'C:\Users\alice becomes /c/Users/alice'; D8's concern was about hook script internals (which receive raw paths), not the `if` evaluation layer

claim: If fnm is absent at WORK locale, all 17 project hooks fail at ensure-fnm.sh before any `if` condition or hook script is reached; block-push-to-main.js and pre-commit-agent-compliance.js fail open
evidence: ensure-fnm.sh exits 1 on missing fnm; both security hooks have continueOnError: false

claim: fnm is NOT in install-cli-tools.sh manifest; WORK locale has no automated fnm installation path
evidence: scripts/install-cli-tools.sh direct read — fnm absent from WINGET_IDS and GITHUB_REPOS arrays

claim: Compound command bypass of `if` conditions: D9's 97.9% figure was from the pre-fix permission engine (GitHub #4956, closed as fixed), NOT from `if` field testing; practical risk is LOW for this project due to 3-layer defense
evidence: GitHub #4956 was about permission allow/deny rules pre-fix; `if` field postdates the fix; block-push-to-main.js has internal regex + permissions.deny fallback both of which are compound-aware

claim: `if` is a performance optimization, not a security boundary — official documentation frames it as avoiding process spawn overhead, not as security enforcement
evidence: Multiple official doc sources frame `if` as optimization; Gemini described it as 'client-side advisory mechanism'; security framing comes from community, not Anthropic

claim: Security research found 84% overall success rate attacking Claude Code hook-based guardrails, rising to 90%+ with attacker adaptation
evidence: Security research gist (hartphoenix, 2026-03); community source, corroborated by official permission docs warning on pattern fragility

claim: HOOK-D5-C / G3-Gate2: A Firestore rules integrity guard is the highest-value new hook proposal; PreToolUse version blocks BEFORE the write; PostToolUse version detects after
evidence: SECURITY.md identifies these three rules as primary defense; post-write-validator.js does not check rules file content; G3 designed the PreToolUse version with full stdin payload analysis

claim: HOOK-D5-A: A pre-deploy safeguard checking out/ freshness and NEXT_PUBLIC_* env vars prevents common stale-build and missing-env deploy mistakes
evidence: deploy-firebase.yml sets 6 env vars from GitHub secrets; local deploy via firebase-service-account.json is possible without CI

claim: Two computed variables in post-write-validator.js (isMarkdownFile line 140, isConfigFile line 142) are never used — dead code representing unfulfilled extension points
evidence: Direct read of post-write-validator.js; grep for references to isMarkdownFile and isConfigFile shows zero usage

claim: P4 (global/gsd-check-update.js) and U1 (~/.claude/hooks/gsd-check-update.js) are duplicate GSD update checks writing to the same cache file — harmless but wasteful
evidence: Both write to ~/.claude/cache/gsd-update-check.json; second write harmlessly overwrites first

claim: The `if` field on PermissionRequest is valid but PermissionRequest hooks do not fire in non-interactive mode (-p flag)
evidence: Official hooks reference note on PermissionRequest event behavior

claim: settings.local.json is committed to git but contains HOME-only permissions (Go binary paths); at WORK these are inert but present, creating misleading appearance of Go availability
evidence: Direct read of settings.local.json; git status shows file is tracked; Go not installed at WORK locale

claim: Pre-commit average duration is 44.4 seconds (64 runs); dominant costs are doc-index (~26s), tests (~28s), eslint (~13s)
evidence: hook-runs.jsonl: 64 pre-commit runs, avg 44,381ms, min 5,290ms, max 147,714ms

claim: Pre-push average duration is ~7.3 seconds (44 runs); dominant costs are circular-deps (~5s), tsc type-check (~5s), hook-tests (~3s)
evidence: hook-runs.jsonl: 44 pre-push runs, avg ~7,300ms

claim: GSD framework (workflows, references, templates, bin) has zero references to gsd-context-monitor — it is an add-on layer, not a GSD dependency
evidence: Exhaustive filesystem search of GSD directory tree found zero matches for gsd-context-monitor or PostToolUse references

claim: gsd-context-monitor.js has a soft dependency on gsd-statusline.js (UserPromptSubmit hook) writing the bridge file; if statusline is removed, monitor silently becomes a no-op
evidence: Script reads /tmp/claude-ctx-{session_id}.json written by statusline; exits at bail-out #2 if file absent

claim: Gemini 2.5 Pro agreed with 7/9 tested claims, partially agreed with 2, and meaningfully disagreed with 1 (post-write-validator splitting viability); overall concordance is high
evidence: G1 detailed verdicts table: AGREE×7, PARTIAL×2, DISAGREE×1, N/A×1 (Gemini CLI failure)

claim: Gemini 2.5 Pro hallucinated Claude Code-specific concepts: 'AfterFileChange' event (not real), 'hookify' plugin (not a Claude Code concept), and CEL expression evaluation in `if` fields (not used)
evidence: G1 claims 5 and 6 detail the hallucinations; none of these concepts appear in official Claude Code documentation

claim: Hooks are NOT hot-reloaded mid-session — changes to settings.json during a session are ignored until the next session start
evidence: G2 empirical test: newly added hook did not fire despite multiple tool calls matching the pattern; GV1 confirmed via official docs: 'changes to hook configuration require restarting Claude Code'

claim: POSIX normalization in `if` field is engine-level, separate from hook command stdin/args — hook scripts still receive Windows-native backslash paths; only the `if` evaluation layer normalizes
evidence: post-write-validator.js line 108 normalizes backslashes internally, proving scripts receive raw Windows paths; official permissions docs confirm `if` evaluation normalizes separately

claim: Settings.json PreToolUse guard is self-bootstrapping: once installed, it fires on any Write/Edit to settings.json and can block removal of itself, creating a self-enforcing protection
evidence: GV1 verified the logic: Write/Edit to settings.json triggers the guard, which checks if critical hooks (including itself) are present in proposed content; exit 2 blocks the operation

claim: Exit code 2 = BLOCK in PreToolUse hooks; exit 0 = allow; any other non-zero = non-blocking error (operation continues)
evidence: Official docs: 'exit code of 2 indicates a blocking error, causing the hook's standard error output to be fed back to Claude, effectively stopping further processing'

claim: .env.local.encrypted unconditional block can be deployed as a 5-minute inline bash one-liner in settings.json requiring no external script
evidence: Inline 'bash -c' commands are valid for 'type: command' hooks; exit 2 + stderr output correctly triggers BLOCK; only steps are: add JSON block + session restart

claim: PostToolUseFailure is a supported hook event that accepts `if` conditions; no existing PostToolUseFailure hooks in this project — the Groundhog Day Loop Detector would be the first
evidence: D1-spec lists PostToolUseFailure in the four `if`-compatible events; settings.json contains no PostToolUseFailure key; GV2 confirmed: 'Runs when a tool execution fails'

claim: Lean fnm wrapper is critical only for T3-1 (Micro-Hook Architecture) among Tier 3 items; T3-2/T3-3/T3-6 are blocked by Windows path issues, T3-4/T3-5 by architectural concerns
evidence: GV2 analysis of all 8 Tier 3 blockers: only T3-1 is spawn-cost blocked; others have Windows path, architecture, or ROI blockers

claim: The `if` field has no access to context window metrics (remaining_percentage, token counts, etc.) — context window data is available only to the statusLine command, a separate system
evidence: statusLine receives rich context_window JSON payload; hook scripts receive only event-specific JSON (tool name, tool input, session ID, cwd); GitHub issues #34340/#34879 request this gap be filled

claim: PreCompact hook fires when context IS full (auto) or when user runs /compact (manual); it does not fire at a configurable threshold — useful for save-state-before-compaction but not for threshold alerting
evidence: Official docs: 'automatically when the context window reaches its capacity'; project already has PreCompact hook in production (pre-compaction-save.js)

claim: block-push-to-main.js has 3-layer compound defense: (a) `if: Bash(git push *)` spawn filter, (b) internal regex `\bgit\b` + `\bpush\b` matching anywhere in full string, (c) permissions.deny compound-aware engine
evidence: GV2 read lines 34-36 and 53-54 of block-push-to-main.js; confirmed \b word boundary and [^|;&]* shell operator exclusion; permissions.deny entry confirmed in settings.json

claim: The permission engine IS compound-aware post-fix: official docs state 'Claude Code is aware of shell operators like &&. A prefix match rule such as Bash(safe-cmd *) will not grant permission to execute a compound command like safe-cmd && other-cmd'
evidence: Direct quote from code.claude.com/docs/en/permissions confirmed via context7; GV2 verified verbatim match

claim: D9's C-033 97.9% bypass figure was from pre-fix permission allow/deny rules (GitHub #4956, closed COMPLETED), not from `if` field testing; the `if` field did not exist when #4956 was filed
evidence: G6 confirmed: GitHub #4956 was about permission engine; issue closed COMPLETED indicating fix was applied; `if` field introduced in v2.1.85 after the fix

claim: For non-code file types (.sh, .yml, .yaml, .jsonl, .md, .json), post-write-validator.js spawns ~234ms to run 0–1 validators; a hybrid approach (monolith for .ts/.tsx, `if`-gated skip for non-code) would be optimal but adds complexity for marginal gain
evidence: GV1 read post-write-validator.js validator gates: .sh and .yml trigger validator 3 only; .jsonl triggers validator 2 only; .md and .json trigger 0 validators; .ts/.tsx trigger 4-6 validators
